member.name: T:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel
member.summary:
Base class for reliable and sequenced communication channels.
Handles the queuing and scheduling of outgoing packets.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.Peer
member.summary: The peer associated with this channel.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.OutgoingQueue
member.summary: Queue containing packets waiting to be sent over the network.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.PacketsInQueue
member.summary: Gets the number of packets currently residing in the outgoing queue.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.#ctor(LiteNetLib.LiteNetPeer)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The peer that owns this channel.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.AddToQueue(LiteNetLib.NetPacket)
member.summary: Adds a packet to the outgoing queue and notifies the peer to schedule a send update.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.AddToPeerChannelSendQueue
member.summary: Thread-safely marks this channel as having pending data and adds it to the peer's update list.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.SendAndCheckQueue
member.summary: Attempts to send packets from the queue. If the queue becomes empty or throttled, it resets the update flag.
member.returns: if there are still packets remaining to be sent in future updates.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.SendNextPackets
member.summary: Abstract method to implement the specific logic for sending packets (e.g., windowing for reliable).
member.returns: if packets were sent and the channel should remain in the send queue.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.BaseChannel.ProcessPacket(LiteNetLib.NetPacket)
member.summary: Abstract method to handle an incoming packet received on this specific channel.
member.returns: if the packet was processed successfully.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.Data
member.summary: Data sent by the remote peer in the connection request.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.RemoteEndPoint
member.summary: The remote endpoint (IP and Port) of the peer requesting the connection.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.AcceptIfKey(System.String)
member.summary: Accepts the connection if the first string in the matches the provided key.
member.remarks: This is a helper method for simple password/key validation. If the key does not match or data is invalid, the connection is automatically rejected.
member.param: The required string key to match.
member.returns: A new if the key matches and connection is accepted; otherwise, null.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.Accept
member.summary: Accept connection and get new NetPeer as result
member.returns: Connected NetPeer

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.Reject(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Rejects the connection request.
member.param: Optional user data to send along with the rejection packet.
Length of the data to be sent from the array.

If , immediately removes the request, if is not a reject packet is also sent.
If , creates a temporary peer that sends rejection packets and lingers in memory until a timeout occurs to handle late-arriving packets.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.Reject(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Rejects the connection reliably. Creates a temporary peer to handle packet loss.
member.param: Data to send with the rejection.
Length of the data to be sent.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.RejectForce(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Rejects the connection immediately without reliability. Minimizes resource usage by not creating an internal peer.
member.param: Data to send with the rejection.
Length of the data to be sent.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.RejectForce
member.summary: Rejects the connection immediately without sending any packet.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.RejectForce(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Rejects the connection immediately without reliability.
member.param: Data to send with the rejection.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteConnectionRequest.RejectForce(LiteNetLib.Utils.NetDataWriter)
member.summary: Rejects the connection immediately without reliability using data from a .
member.param: Writer containing the data to send.

member.summary: Rejects the connection reliably without additional data.

member.summary: Rejects the connection reliably.
member.param: Data to send with the rejection.

member.summary: Rejects the connection reliably using data from a .

member.name: T:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager
member.summary: Main class for all network operations. Can be used as client and/or server.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager._dropPacket
member.summary: Used with and to tag packets that need to be dropped. Only relevant when DEBUG is defined.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.UnconnectedMessagesEnabled
member.summary: Enable messages receiving without connection. (with SendUnconnectedMessage method)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.NatPunchEnabled
member.summary: Enable nat punch messages

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.UpdateTime
member.summary: Library logic update and send period in milliseconds Lowest values in Windows doesn't change much because of Thread.Sleep precision To more frequent sends (or sends tied to your game logic) use

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.PingInterval
member.summary: Interval for latency detection and checking connection (in milliseconds)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.DisconnectTimeout
member.summary: If NetManager doesn't receive any packet from remote peer during this time (in milliseconds) then connection will be closed (including library internal keepalive packets)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.SimulatePacketLoss
member.summary: Simulate packet loss by dropping random amount of packets. (Works only in DEBUG builds or when SIMULATE_NETWORK is defined)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.SimulateLatency
member.summary: Simulate latency by holding packets for random time. (Works only in DEBUG builds or when SIMULATE_NETWORK is defined)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.SimulationPacketLossChance
member.summary: Chance of packet loss when simulation enabled. value in percents (1 - 100).

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.SimulationMinLatency
member.summary: Minimum simulated round-trip latency (in milliseconds). Actual latency applied per direction is half of this value.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.SimulationMaxLatency
member.summary: Maximum simulated round-trip latency (in milliseconds). Actual latency applied per direction is half of this value.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.UnsyncedEvents
member.summary: Events automatically will be called without PollEvents method from another thread

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.UnsyncedReceiveEvent
member.summary: If true - receive event will be called from "receive" thread immediately otherwise on PollEvents call

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.UnsyncedDeliveryEvent
member.summary: If true - delivery event will be called from "receive" thread immediately otherwise on PollEvents call

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.BroadcastReceiveEnabled
member.summary: Allows receive broadcast packets

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.ReconnectDelay
member.summary: Delay between initial connection attempts (in milliseconds)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.MaxConnectAttempts
member.summary: Maximum connection attempts before client stops and call disconnect event.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.ReuseAddress
member.summary: Enables socket option "ReuseAddress" for specific purposes

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.DontRoute
member.summary: UDP Only Socket Option Normally IP sockets send packets of data through routers and gateways until they reach the final destination. If the DontRoute flag is set to True, then data will be delivered on the local subnet only.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.Statistics
member.summary: Statistics of all connections

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.EnableStatistics
member.summary: Toggles the collection of network statistics for the instance and all known peers

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.MaxFragmentsCount
member.summary: Max fragmented packets size for reliable channels - that equals to data of size fragments count * (MTU-reliable header size)

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.NatPunchModule
member.summary: NatPunchModule for NAT hole punching operations

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.IsRunning
member.summary: Returns true if socket listening and update thread is running

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.LocalPort
member.summary: Local EndPoint (host and port)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.AutoRecycle
member.summary: Automatically recycle NetPacketReader after OnReceive event

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.IPv6Enabled
member.summary: IPv6 support

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.MtuOverride
member.summary: Override MTU for all new peers registered in this NetManager, will ignores MTU Discovery!

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.MtuDiscovery
member.summary: Automatically discovery mtu starting from. Use at own risk because some routers can break MTU detection and connection in result

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.FirstPeer
member.summary: First peer. Useful for Client mode

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.UseNativeSockets
member.summary: Experimental feature mostly for servers. Only for Windows/Linux use direct socket calls for send/receive to drastically increase speed and reduce GC pressure

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.DisconnectOnUnreachable
member.summary: Disconnect peers if HostUnreachable or NetworkUnreachable spawned (old behaviour 0.9.x was true)

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.AllowPeerAddressChange
member.summary: Allows peer change it's ip (lte to wifi, wifi to lte, etc). Use only on server

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.ConnectedPeersCount
member.summary: Returns connected peers count

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.MaxPacketPerManualReceive
member.summary: Maximum packets that can be received per one ManualReceive (PollEvents when started using StartInManualMode) 0 - infinite - but this can cause some big delays there is too many incoming packets

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.ExtraPacketSizeForLayer
member.summary: Gets the additional size in bytes required by the active .
member.remarks:
This value is used by to calculate the available MTU for user data.
If a packet layer is active (e.g., for encryption or CRC), this returns the overhead added to every packet. Returns 0 if no packet layer is assigned.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.#ctor(LiteNetLib.ILiteNetEventListener,LiteNetLib.Layers.PacketLayerBase)
member.summary: NetManager constructor
member.param: Network events listener (also can implement IDeliveryEventListener)
Extra processing of packages, like CRC checksum or encryption. All connected NetManagers must have same layer.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.DisconnectPeer(LiteNetLib.LiteNetPeer,LiteNetLib.DisconnectReason,System.Net.Sockets.SocketError,System.Boolean,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,LiteNetLib.NetPacket)
member.summary: Disconnects a peer and handles internal state cleanup.
member.param: The reason for disconnection provided to the event listener.
The error code from the underlying socket, if any.

If , immediately sets state to without sending a notification.
If , sends unreliable disconnect packets until and sets state to . Peer will linger until to ignore late-arriving packets from the old session.
Optional custom data to include in the disconnect packet.
Number of bytes to send from the array.
Internal packet data associated with the disconnect event.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteNetManager.ManualUpdate(System.Single)
member.summary: Updates internal peer states, handles timeouts, processes NTP requests and sends buffered packets.
member.param: Time passed since the last update frame.
member.remarks: Must be called continuously from the main loop if was set to .

member.summary: Send data to all connected peers (channel - 0)

member.summary: Send data to all connected peers (channel - 0)

member.summary: Send data to all connected peers (channel - 0)

member.summary: Send data to all connected peers (channel - 0)

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteNetPeer.Shutdown(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Internally handles the shutdown process for this peer.
member.param: Optional data to include in the unreliable disconnect packet.
Length of the data to send.

If , immediately sets state to without sending a notification.
If , sends unreliable disconnect packets until a timeout occurs and sets state to Queued reliable packets are bypassed and dropped immediately.
member.returns: A indicating the state change transition.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.LiteNetPeer.ProcessConnectRequest(LiteNetLib.NetConnectRequestPacket)
member.summary: Evaluates incoming connection requests against the current peer state to supply Reconnect Protection.
member.returns: A directing how the manager should handle the request.
member.remarks: If the state is , the peer lingers to ignore older connection requests (where the packet timestamp is smaller than internal ), ensuring older connections are ignored.

member.name: T:LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod
member.summary: Sending method type

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod.Unreliable
member.summary: Unreliable. Packets can be dropped, can be duplicated, can arrive without order.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod.ReliableUnordered
member.summary: Reliable. Packets won't be dropped, won't be duplicated, can arrive without order.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod.Sequenced
member.summary: Unreliable. Packets can be dropped, won't be duplicated, will arrive in order.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod.ReliableOrdered
member.summary: Reliable and ordered. Packets won't be dropped, won't be duplicated, will arrive in order.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.DeliveryMethod.ReliableSequenced
member.summary: Reliable only last packet. Packets can be dropped (except the last one), won't be duplicated, will arrive in order. Cannot be fragmented

member.name: T:LiteNetLib.NetConstants
member.summary: Network constants. Can be tuned from sources for your purposes.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.DefaultWindowSize
member.summary: Default window size for reliable channels (number of packets).

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.SocketBufferSize
member.summary:
Size of the underlying UDP socket receive and send buffers in bytes.
Default is 1MB.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.SocketTTL
member.summary: Time To Live (TTL) for the UDP packets.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.HeaderSize
member.summary: Size of the base packet header (PacketProperty) in s.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.ChanneledHeaderSize
member.summary:
Size of the header for sequenced or reliable messages in s.
Includes , Sequence, and ChannelId.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.FragmentHeaderSize
member.summary:
Additional header size required for fragmented packets in s.
Includes FragmentId, FragmentPart, and FragmentsTotal.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.FragmentedHeaderTotalSize
member.summary:
Total header size for a fragmented channeled packet in s.
Combines and .

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.MaxSequence
member.summary: Maximum possible sequence number before wrapping back to zero.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.HalfMaxSequence
member.summary: Half of the , used for sequence comparison and wrap-around logic.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.InitialMtu
member.summary: The starting Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) used for new connections before path MTU discovery.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.MaxPacketSize
member.summary: Maximum possible packet size allowed by the library based on the largest supported MTU.

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.MaxUnreliableDataSize
member.summary:
Maximum payload size for a single unreliable packet in s.
Calculated as - .

member.name: F:LiteNetLib.NetConstants.MaxConnectionNumber
member.summary: Maximum possible value for .
member.remarks:
This value is used to distinguish between different connection instances from the same .
It allows the receiver to identify and discard packets belonging to previous connection attempts that may arrive late due to network jitter, even if they originate from the same address and port.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.Property
member.summary: Gets or sets the packet property (type). Stored in the first 5 bits of the first byte (0x1F mask).

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.ConnectionNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets the connection number used to distinguish between multiple connection instances from the same endpoint. Stored in bits 6 and 7 of the first byte (0x60 mask).
member.remarks: Used to discard packets from previous connections made in the same frame/time.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.Sequence
member.summary: Gets or sets the sequence number of the packet. Located at offset 1 in .

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.IsFragmented
member.summary: Returns if the fragmentation bit (the highest bit of the first byte) is set.

member.name: M:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.MarkFragmented
member.summary: Sets the fragmentation bit (0x80) in the packet header.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.ChannelId
member.summary: Gets or sets the channel identifier. Located at offset 3 in .

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.FragmentId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for a fragmented message. Located at offset 4 in .

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.FragmentPart
member.summary: Gets or sets the index of the current fragment part. Located at offset 6 in .

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.NetPacket.FragmentsTotal
member.summary: Gets or sets the total number of fragments in the message. Located at offset 8 in .

member.name: T:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket
member.summary: Represents RFC4330 SNTP packet used for communication to and from a network time server.

remarks.para: Most applications should just use the property.
The same data structure represents both request and reply packets. Request and reply differ in which properties are set and to what values.

The only real property is . All other properties read from and write to the underlying byte array with the exception of , which is not part of the packet on network and it is instead set locally after receiving the packet.
Copied from GuerrillaNtp project with permission from Robert Vazan (@robertvazan) under MIT license, see https://github.com/RevenantX/LiteNetLib/pull/236

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.Bytes
member.summary: Gets RFC4330-encoded SNTP packet.
member.value: Byte array containing RFC4330-encoded SNTP packet. It is at least 48 bytes long.
member.remarks: This is the only real property. All other properties except read from or write to this byte array.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.LeapIndicator
member.summary: Gets the leap second indicator.
member.value: Leap second warning, if any. Special value indicates unsynchronized server clock. Default is .
member.remarks: Only servers fill in this property. Clients can consult this property for possible leap second warning.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.VersionNumber
member.summary: Gets or sets protocol version number.
member.value: SNTP protocol version. Default is 4, which is the latest version at the time of this writing.
member.remarks: In request packets, clients should leave this property at default value 4. Servers usually reply with the same protocol version.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.Mode
member.summary: Gets or sets SNTP packet mode, i.e. whether this is client or server packet.
member.value: SNTP packet mode. Default is in newly created packets. Server reply should have this property set to .

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.Stratum
member.summary: Gets server's distance from the reference clock.

value.para: Distance from the reference clock. This property is set only in server reply packets. Servers connected directly to reference clock hardware set this property to 1. Statum number is incremented by 1 on every hop down the NTP server hierarchy.
Special value 0 indicates that this packet is a Kiss-o'-Death message with kiss code stored in .

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.Poll
member.summary: Gets server's preferred polling interval.
member.value: Polling interval in log2 seconds, e.g. 4 stands for 16s and 17 means 131,072s.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.Precision
member.summary: Gets the precision of server clock.
member.value: Clock precision in log2 seconds, e.g. -20 for microsecond precision.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.RootDelay
member.summary: Gets the total round-trip delay from the server to the reference clock.
member.value: Round-trip delay to the reference clock. Normally a positive value smaller than one second.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.RootDispersion
member.summary: Gets the estimated error in time reported by the server.
member.value: Estimated error in time reported by the server. Normally a positive value smaller than one second.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.ReferenceId
member.summary: Gets the ID of the time source used by the server or Kiss-o'-Death code sent by the server.

value.para: ID of server's time source or Kiss-o'-Death code. Purpose of this property depends on value of property.
Stratum 1 servers write here one of several special values that describe the kind of hardware clock they use.
Stratum 2 and lower servers set this property to IPv4 address of their upstream server. If upstream server has IPv6 address, the address is hashed, because it doesn't fit in this property.
When server sets to special value 0, this property contains so called kiss code that instructs the client to stop querying the server.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.ReferenceTimestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the time when the server clock was last set or corrected.
member.value: Time when the server clock was last set or corrected or null when not specified.
member.remarks: This Property is usually set only by servers. It usually lags server's current time by several minutes, so don't use this property for time synchronization.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.OriginTimestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the time when the client sent its request.
member.value: This property is null in request packets. In reply packets, it is the time when the client sent its request. Servers copy this value from that they find in received request packet.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.ReceiveTimestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the time when the request was received by the server.
member.value: This property is null in request packets. In reply packets, it is the time when the server received client request.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.TransmitTimestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the time when the packet was sent.
member.value: Time when the packet was sent. It should never be null. Default value is .
member.remarks: This property must be set by both clients and servers.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.DestinationTimestamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the time of reception of response SNTP packet on the client.
member.value: Time of reception of response SNTP packet on the client. It is null in request packets.
member.remarks: This property is not part of the protocol and has to be set when reply packet is received.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.RoundTripTime
member.summary: Gets the round-trip time to the server.
member.value: Time the request spent traveling to the server plus the time the reply spent traveling back. This is calculated from timestamps in the packet as (t1 - t0) + (t3 - t2) where t0 is , t1 is , t2 is , and t3 is . This property throws an exception in request packets.

member.name: P:LiteNetLib.Utils.NtpPacket.CorrectionOffset
member.summary: Gets the offset that should be added to local time to synchronize it with server time.
member.value: Time difference between server and client. It should be added to local time to get server time. It is calculated from timestamps in the packet as 0.5 * ((t1 - t0) - (t3 - t2)) where t0 is , t1 is , t2 is , and t3 is . This property throws an exception in request packets.
